prompt:
Integration-ops ticket. Our SAP Cloud Integration tenant runs ~40 productive iflows and the on-call rotation still checks the Monitor UI by hand; last Saturday a FAILED spike on Replicate_Cost_Centers sat unnoticed for six hours. sap/client.ts in this repo already speaks the tenant's OData API (basic auth, one page of MessageProcessingLogs, OData error decode) and its behavior stays as-is; build the monitoring layer on top. test_cpi_monitor.ts is the acceptance contract — it and everything under docs/ are protected, and the existing client behavior it re-checks must keep working.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official SAP documentation for the Cloud Integration OData API — the query-options page for MessageProcessingLogs (which $-options the API supports, the exact $filter examples, and the two traps: what the docs say about time-zone information in datetime literals, and what happens when a query would return more than the per-call cap), the HTTP-calls/URI-components page for how the service root is composed, the message-status page for the full documented Status enum, and enough of the OData 2.0 JSON format spec (which SAP's docs defer to) to know how collections, counts, next links, deferred navigation properties, and Edm.DateTime values are represented on the wire. First-party sources only: help.sap.com / SAP's official SAP-docs GitHub mirror / api.sap.com / odata.org for the protocol SAP pins. docs/contract.json records the wire contract our mock speaks and docs/official_sources.json its provenance; if the live docs contradict a pin, say so in your summary and leave the protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Feature work (tests import both modules):
- New sap/monitor.ts exporting buildLogsQuery(opts), listAllLogs(client, opts), normalizeLog(raw), fetchErrorText(client, raw), plus MonitorError and AuthorizationError. buildLogsQuery serializes {status, customStatus, integrationFlowName, logStartAfter, logEndBefore, orderBy: {field, desc}, top, skip, countAll} into the exact query string the tests pin — mind the option order, the space encoding, and especially what the docs say datetime literals may and may not contain (our ops scripts pass ISO strings with Z or +hh:mm offsets; the API will silently mis-read anything you forward blindly).
- listAllLogs follows the server's continuation mechanism the way the OData 2.0 spec says (our mock plants a marker param in the continuation URL that a rebuilt URL loses), sends credentials on every hop, returns normalized entries plus the total count as a number when countAll was requested (null otherwise).
- normalizeLog maps the wire entry to {messageGuid, correlationId, flowName, status, customStatus, logStart, logEnd, sender, receiver} with the /Date(ms)/ wire dates turned into ISO-8601 UTC strings (logEnd can legitimately be missing while a message is in flight — represent that as null, not an epoch). Statuses outside the documented enum are a data bug upstream: throw MonitorError naming the offending value. CustomStatus is not Status — keep both.
- fetchErrorText: failed logs carry a navigation property that arrives deferred; read the error text the way SAP's docs show for error-information resources, in a single request, and produce no API traffic at all when the entry has no such link.
- AuthorizationError distinguishes the two documented authorization failures (bad credentials vs missing role) with kind/status/code fields; heads-up, the existing client only raises on responses that carry an OData error body, and one of the two failure modes doesn't. Never put credentials or the Authorization header value into any error message.

Acceptance: node --test test_cpi_monitor.ts (Node built-ins only, no npm installs). Loopback mock only, dummy credentials, no sleeps, no wall-clock dependence.

research.product: SAP Cloud Integration (SAP Integration Suite) — OData API, Message Processing Logs
research.interface: OData API v2 at <tenant>/api/v1, MessageProcessingLogs API 1.0.0 (OData 2.0)

official_sources.title: Query Options | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror of help.sap.com)
official_sources.used_for: supported system query options ($filter/$select/$orderby/$top/$skip/$inlinecount/$expand); MPL $filter examples (Status eq 'ERROR', LogEnd gt datetime'...' and LogStart lt datetime'...'); 'parsing of time zone information in datetime literals is not supported. All datetime literals are interpreted as UTC'; server-side paging: max 1000 entries per call, responses over the cap carry a next link with $skiptoken; browser %20 space-encoding note

official_sources.title: HTTP Calls and URI Components | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: service root URI = service-key url parameter + /api/v1; call = service root + resource path + query options; example /api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs?$filter=Status eq 'COMPLETED'

official_sources.title: Message Processing Logs | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: MPL resources (Logs, Error Information, Custom Header Properties, Adapter Attributes); MonitoringDataRead role template for API clients; DISCARDED logs carry no error information; X-CSRF-Token: Fetch flow for modifying calls

official_sources.title: Message Status | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: the complete documented Status enum: COMPLETED, PROCESSING, RETRY, ESCALATED, FAILED, CANCELLED, DISCARDED, ABANDONED

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Message Processing Logs (Version 1.0.0)
official_sources.used_for: API name/version (MessageProcessingLogs 1.0.0), 'implemented based on OData version 2 specification', service URL shape .../api/v1

official_sources.title: JSON Format (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: d wrapper; results array for collections; __next partial-set link semantics; __deferred navigation representation; /Date(ms)/ Edm.DateTime encoding

official_sources.title: Runtime Artifacts and Error Information Example Requests | SAP Cloud Integration (SAP-docs official mirror)
official_sources.used_for: the .../ErrorInformation/$value pattern returning the error text for an entity

research.language_rationale: The ops tooling this monitor extends is TypeScript on Node (sap/client.ts ships here); SAP documents the Cloud Integration API as plain OData 2.0 over HTTP with basic/OAuth auth and d-envelope JSON, and SAP publishes no supported Node SDK for this API, so Node's built-in fetch against the documented wire contract is the credible dependency-free surface.
